chromium_dump_dom.observed: DOM output file remained empty; Chromium emitted sandbox/dbus/zygote errors and did not complete reliably.

chromium_screenshot.observed: Chromium timed out under multiple flag sets, including single-process/no-zygote.

playwright_cli.observed: Playwright CLI is installed but its browser bundle is not installed in the runtime cache.

weasyprint_pdf_png_proxy.command_family: weasyprint HTML->PDF plus PDF render to PNG
weasyprint_pdf_png_proxy.observed: Renderer proxy generated nonblank PDF and PNG render pages.

human_visual_review_prompts.prompt: After viewing the tracker briefly, can the operator tell whether the OS is bootable, what stage is active, and what decision comes next?
Is the next safe action visually obvious without reading the whole page?
Would this surface make the teacher/operator look prepared, credible, and intentional if shown to another adult?
Can status be understood without color alone, and are focus/reduced-motion/mobile requirements visible in the implementation?
At phone width, do the status cards stack cleanly and preserve the main answer above the fold?

limitations: No true Chromium/Playwright screenshot could be completed in this sandbox turn; WeasyPrint/PDF/PNG render is an independent proxy, not a browser-layout substitute.

promotion_decision.required_before_full_authority_export: A future browser-backed render proof should be run when Chromium or Playwright browser binaries are reliable; current stage includes proxy render proof plus static gate checks.
